statement: Application-thrown errors should be instances of typed error classes that extend a base `AppError`, not raw `Error` or string throws.
structure: AppError (base) → NotFoundError, ValidationError, AuthorizationError, ConflictError, etc. Each type carries a stable error code and a serializable shape suitable for the API response layer. The base class handles serialization once; subclasses add fields specific to their kind.
why: Centralizes API error formatting in one place (the error-to-response middleware). Makes error handling discriminable in catch blocks (instanceof checks instead of string-matching error messages). Makes errors testable — you can assert on the type, not the wording. Decouples internal error semantics from external API contract.
anti_pattern: Throwing strings, throwing plain Error with message-based discrimination, or returning a `{ ok: false, error: '...' }` shape from internal functions and converting at every boundary.
